tafsir.siraj-ar: إِنَّ إِبۡرَٰهِيمَ كَانَ أُمَّةٗ قَانِتٗا لِّلَّهِ حَنِيفٗا وَلَمۡ يَكُ مِنَ ٱلۡمُشۡرِكِينَ (أُمَّةً: إِمَامًا، جَامِعًا لِخِصَالِ الخَيْرِ, قَانِتًا: خَاضِعًا، مُدَاوِمًا عَلَى الطَّاعَةِ, حَنِيفًا: مَائِلًا عَنِ الشِّرْكِ إِلَى التَّوْحِيدِ قَصْدًا)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Sesungguhnya Ibrahim adalah seorang imam) seorang penghulu yang menjadi panutan dan di dalam dirinya terkandung semua akhlak yang baik (lagi patuh) sangat taat (kepada Allah dan hanif) cenderung kepada agama yang lurus. (Dan sekali-kali dia bukanlah termasuk orang-orang yang mempersekutukan Tuhan)

ar: شَاكِرࣰا لِّأَنۡعُمِهِۚ ٱجۡتَبَىٰهُ وَهَدَىٰهُ إِلَىٰ صِرَ ٰطࣲ مُّسۡتَقِیمࣲ
ar_simple: شاكرا لأنعمه ٱجتبىه وهدىه إلى صر ط مستقیم

t.sahih-int: [He was] grateful for His favors. Allah chose him and guided him to a straight path
t.asad: [for he was always] grateful for the blessings granted by Him who had elected him and guided him onto a straight way
t.yusuf-ali: He showed his gratitude for the favours of Allah, who chose him, and guided him to a Straight Way
t.pickthall: Thankful for His bounties; He chose him and He guided him unto a straight path
t.arberry: showing thankfulness for His blessings; He chose him, and He guided him to a straight path
t.hilali-khan: (He was) thankful for His (Allah's) Graces. He (Allah) chose him (as an intimate friend) and guided him to a Straight Path (Islamic Monotheism, neither Judaism nor Christianity)
t.itani: Thankful for His blessings. He chose him, and guided him to a straight path.
t.mubarakpuri: (He was) thankful for His favors. He (Allah) chose him and guided him to a straight path.
t.qarib: He was grateful for the grace poured out on him by God-God had chosen him and guided him along a straight path
t.wahiduddin: he was thankful for His blessings. God chose him and guided him to a straight path
t.shakir: Grateful for His favors; He chose him and guided him on the right path
t.haleem: he was thankful for the blessings of God who chose him and guided him to a straight path
t.maariful: He was grateful to His bounties. He (Allah) chose him and led him to the straight path
t.khattab: ˹utterly˺ grateful for Allah’s favours. ˹So˺ He chose him and guided him to the Straight Path
t.ghali: Thankful for His amenities. He selected him and He guided him to a straight Path
t.irving: grateful for His favors, He chose and guided him to a Straight Road
t.iwq: He was grateful for His favors. Allah chose him and guided him to a straight path

t_id.kemenag: (lagi) yang mensyukuri nikmat-nikmat Allah. Allah telah memilihnya dan menunjukinya kepada jalan yang lurus.
t_id.sabiq: Ibrâhîm senantiasa bersyukur atas nikmat-nikmat Tuhan yang telah diberikan kepadanya. Dengan alasan itulah Allah memilih Ibrâhîm sebagai pengemban misi risalah-Nya. Allah merestui Ibrâhîm untuk meniti jalan lurus menuju kebahagiaan abadi.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Lagi yang mensyukuri nikmat-nikmat Allah, Allah telah memilihnya) menjadikannya sebagai pilihan-Nya (dan menunjukkannya kepada jalan yang lurus.)
t_id.iwq: (lagi) yang mensyukuri nikmat-nikmat Allah. Allah telah memilihnya dan menunjukinya kepada jalan yang lurus.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «شاكرا لأنعمه اجتباه» اصطفاه «وهداهُ إلى صراط مستقيم»
tafsir.siraj-ar: شَاكِرٗا لِّأَنۡعُمِهِۚ ٱجۡتَبَىٰهُ وَهَدَىٰهُ إِلَىٰ صِرَٰطٖ مُّسۡتَقِيمٖ (اجْتَبَاهُ: اخْتَارَهُ)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Lagi yang mensyukuri nikmat-nikmat Allah, Allah telah memilihnya) menjadikannya sebagai pilihan-Nya (dan menunjukkannya kepada jalan yang lurus)

ar: وَءَاتَیۡنَـٰهُ فِی ٱلدُّنۡیَا حَسَنَةࣰۖ وَإِنَّهُۥ فِی ٱلۡءَاخِرَةِ لَمِنَ ٱلصَّـٰلِحِینَ
ar_simple: وءاتینه فی ٱلدنیا حسنة وإنه فی ٱلءاخرة لمن ٱلصلحین

t.sahih-int: And We gave him good in this world, and indeed, in the Hereafter he will be among the righteous
t.asad: And so We vouchsafed him good in this world; and, verily, in the life to come [too] he shall find himself among the righteous
t.yusuf-ali: And We gave him Good in this world, and he will be, in the Hereafter, in the ranks of the Righteous
t.pickthall: And We gave him good in the world, and in the Hereafter he is among the righteous
t.arberry: And We gave him in this world good, and in the world to come he shall be among the righteous
t.hilali-khan: And We gave him good in this world, and in the Hereafter he shall be of the righteous
t.itani: And We gave him goodness in this world, and in the Hereafter he will be among the righteous.
t.mubarakpuri: And We gave him good in this world, and in the Hereafter he shall be of the righteous.
t.qarib: And so We granted him favor in this world: truly, in the life to come he will be among the righteous
t.wahiduddin: We gave him blessings in this world, and in the Hereafter he shall be among the righteous
t.shakir: And We gave him good in this world, and in the next he will most surely be among the good
t.haleem: We gave him blessings in this world, and he is among the right-eous in the Hereafter
t.maariful: We bestowed good upon him in this world; and in the Hereafter, he is among the righteous
t.khattab: We blessed him with all goodness in this world, and in the Hereafter he will certainly be among the righteous
t.ghali: And We brought him in the present (life) (Literally: the lowly "life", i.e., the life of this world) a fair (reward), and surely in the Hereafter he will indeed be among the righteous
t.irving: We gave him a fine thing in this world, and he will be among the honorable in the Hereafter
t.iwq: And We gave him good in this world, and verily, in the Hereafter he will be among the righteous

t_id.kemenag: Dan Kami berikan kepadanya kebaikan di dunia. Dan sesungguhnya dia di akhirat benar-benar termasuk orang-orang yang saleh.
t_id.sabiq: Kami jadikan Ibrâhîm sebagai sosok yang dikenang kebaikan-kebaikannya oleh seluruh manusia. Dan di akhirat nanti ia akan termasuk golongan orang saleh dan akan mendapat kehidupan yang menyenangkan dengan perkenan Allah.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Dan Kami berikan kepadanya) di dalam ungkapan ini terkandung pengertian iltifat dari gaibah, atau perpindahan ungkapan dari dhamir gaib kepada dhamir mutakallim (kebaikan di dunia) yaitu mendapatkan pujian dari kalangan semua agama. (Dan sesungguhnya dia di akhirat benar-benar termasuk orang-orang yang saleh) yaitu orang-orang yang memiliki derajat yang tinggi di akhirat.
t_id.iwq: Dan Kami berikan kepadanya kebaikan di dunia. Dan sesungguhnya dia di akhirat benar-benar termasuk orang-orang yang saleh.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «وآتيناه» فيه التفات عن الغيبة «في الدنيا حسنة» هي الثناء الحسن في كل أهل الأديان «وإنه في الآخرة لمن الصالحين» الذين لهم الدرجات العلى
tafsir.siraj-ar: وَءَاتَيۡنَٰهُ فِي ٱلدُّنۡيَا حَسَنَةٗۖ وَإِنَّهُۥ فِي ٱلۡأٓخِرَةِ لَمِنَ ٱلصَّـٰلِحِينَ
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Dan Kami berikan kepadanya) di dalam ungkapan ini terkandung pengertian iltifat dari gaibah, atau perpindahan ungkapan dari dhamir gaib kepada dhamir mutakallim (kebaikan di dunia) yaitu mendapatkan pujian dari kalangan semua agama. (Dan sesungguhnya dia di akhirat benar-benar termasuk orang-orang yang saleh) yaitu orang-orang yang memiliki derajat yang tinggi di akhirat

ar: ثُمَّ أَوۡحَیۡنَاۤ إِلَیۡكَ أَنِ ٱتَّبِعۡ مِلَّةَ إِبۡرَ ٰهِیمَ حَنِیفࣰاۖ وَمَا كَانَ مِنَ ٱلۡمُشۡرِكِینَ
ar_simple: ثم أوحینا إلیك أن ٱتبع ملة إبر هیم حنیفا وما كان من ٱلمشركین

t.sahih-int: Then We revealed to you, [O Muhammad], to follow the religion of Abraham, inclining toward truth; and he was not of those who associate with Allah
t.asad: And lastly, We have inspired thee, [O Muhammad, with this message:] "Follow the creed of Abraham, who turned away from all that is false, and was not of those who ascribe divinity to aught beside God
t.yusuf-ali: So We have taught thee the inspired (Message), "Follow the ways of Abraham the True in Faith, and he joined not gods with Allah
t.pickthall: And afterward We inspired thee (Muhammad, saying): Follow the religion of Abraham, as one by nature upright. He was not of the idolaters
t.arberry: Then We revealed to thee: 'Follow thou the creed of Abraham, a man of pure faith and no idolater
t.hilali-khan: Then, We have inspired you (O Muhammad SAW saying): "Follow the religion of Ibrahim (Abraham) Hanifa (Islamic Monotheism - to worship none but Allah) and he was not of the Mushrikun (polytheists, idolaters, disbelievers, etc)
t.itani: Then We inspired you: “Follow the religion of Abraham, the Monotheist. He was not an idol-worshiper.”
t.mubarakpuri: Then, We have sent the revelation to you: "Follow the religion of Ibrahim (he was a) Hanif, and he was not one of the idolators."
t.qarib: And We have given you inspiration, [Prophet]: "Follow the faith of Abraham, who turned from every false thing, and who was not among the idolaters
t.wahiduddin: Then We revealed Our will to you [O Muhammad], saying, "Follow the religion of Abraham, the upright in faith; he was not one of the polytheists
t.shakir: Then We revealed to you: Follow the faith of Ibrahim, the upright one, and he was not of the polytheists
t.haleem: Then We revealed to you [Muhammad], ‘Follow the creed of Abraham, a man of pure faith who was not an idolater.’
t.maariful: Then, We revealed to you, “Follow the way of Ibrāhīm, the upright, and he was not among the Mushriks”
t.khattab: Then We revealed to you ˹O Prophet, saying˺: “Follow the faith of Abraham, the upright, who was not one of the polytheists.”
t.ghali: Thereafter We revealed to you, (saying): "Closely follow the creed of Ibrahim, (Abraham) unswervingly upright, and in no way was he one of the associators." (Those who associate others with Allah)
t.irving: Then we inspired you: "Follow the sect of Abraham the Seeker; he was no associator [of others in his worship of God]
t.iwq: Then We revealed to you, O Muhammad, to follow the religion of Abraham, inclining toward truth; and he was not of those who associate with Allah

t_id.kemenag: Kemudian Kami wahyukan kepadamu (Muhammad): "Ikutilah agama Ibrahim seorang yang hanif" dan bukanlah dia termasuk orang-orang yang mempersekutukan Tuhan.
t_id.sabiq: Selang beberapa abad sesudah itu, Kami mewahyukan kepadamu, wahai Muhammad, untuk mengikuti misi kerasulan Ibrâhîm yang berintikan pada ajaran tawhid, moral dan menjauhi kepercayaan-kepercayaan palsu. Sebab Ibrâhîm bukan termasuk golongan yang menyekutukan Allah sebagaimana dakwaan orang-orang musyrik.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Kemudian Kami wahyukan kepadamu) hai Muhammad (ikutilah millah) yakni agama (Ibrahim seorang yang hanif. Dan bukanlah dia termasuk orang-orang yang mempersekutukan Tuhan) Allah swt. mengulangi ayat ini untuk menyanggah anggapan orang-orang Yahudi dan orang-orang Nasrani yang mengakui bahwa Nabi Ibrahim adalah pemeluk agama mereka.
t_id.iwq: Kemudian Kami wahyukan kepadamu (Muhammad): "Ikutilah agama Ibrahim seorang yang hanif" dan bukanlah dia termasuk orang-orang yang mempersekutukan Tuhan.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «ثم أوحينا إليك» دينه يا محمد «أن اتبع ملة» دينه «إبراهيم حنيفا وما كان من المشركين» كرر ردا على زعم اليهود والنصارى أنهم على دينه
tafsir.siraj-ar: ثُمَّ أَوۡحَيۡنَآ إِلَيۡكَ أَنِ ٱتَّبِعۡ مِلَّةَ إِبۡرَٰهِيمَ حَنِيفٗاۖ وَمَا كَانَ مِنَ ٱلۡمُشۡرِكِينَ
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Kemudian Kami wahyukan kepadamu) hai Muhammad (ikutilah millah) yakni agama (Ibrahim seorang yang hanif. Dan bukanlah dia termasuk orang-orang yang mempersekutukan Tuhan) Allah swt. mengulangi ayat ini untuk menyanggah anggapan orang-orang Yahudi dan orang-orang Nasrani yang mengakui bahwa Nabi Ibrahim adalah pemeluk agama mereka

ar: إِنَّمَا جُعِلَ ٱلسَّبۡتُ عَلَى ٱلَّذِینَ ٱخۡتَلَفُوا۟ فِیهِۚ وَإِنَّ رَبَّكَ لَیَحۡكُمُ بَیۡنَهُمۡ یَوۡمَ ٱلۡقِیَـٰمَةِ فِیمَا كَانُوا۟ فِیهِ یَخۡتَلِفُونَ
ar_simple: إنما جعل ٱلسبت على ٱلذین ٱختلفوا فیه وإن ربك لیحكم بینهم یوم ٱلقیمة فیما كانوا فیه یختلفون

t.sahih-int: The sabbath was only appointed for those who differed over it. And indeed, your Lord will judge between them on the Day of Resurrection concerning that over which they used to differ
t.asad: [and know that the observance of] the Sabbath was ordained only for those who came to hold divergent views about him; but, verily, God will judge between them on Resurrection Day with regard to all on which they were wont to differ
t.yusuf-ali: The Sabbath was only made (strict) for those who disagreed (as to its observance); But Allah will judge between them on the Day of Judgment, as to their differences
t.pickthall: The Sabbath was appointed only for those who differed concerning it, and lo! thy Lord will judge between them on the Day of Resurrection concerning that wherein they used to differ
t.arberry: The Sabbath was only appointed for those who were at variance thereon; surely thy Lord will decide between them on the Day of Resurrection, touching their differences
t.hilali-khan: The Sabbath was only prescribed for those who differed concerning it, and verily, your Lord will judge between them on the Day of Resurrection about that wherein they used to differ
t.itani: The Sabbath was decreed only for those who differed about it. Your Lord will judge between them on the Day of Resurrection regarding their differences.
t.mubarakpuri: The Sabbath was only prescribed for those who differed concerning it, and verily, your Lord will judge between them on the Day of Resurrection about what they differed over.
t.qarib: The Sabbath was made obligatory only on those who differed about it. Your Lord will judge between them on the Day of Resurrection regarding to all matters about which they differed
t.wahiduddin: The Sabbath was only enjoined on those who differed about it. Your Lord will judge between them on the Day of Resurrection regarding the things about which they differed
t.shakir: The Sabbath was ordained only for those who differed about it, and most surely your Lord will judge between them on the resurrection day concerning that about which they differed
t.haleem: The Sabbath was made obligatory only for those who differed about it. On the Day of Resurrection your Lord will judge between them as to their differences
t.maariful: The Sabbath was prescribed only for those people (i.e. Jews) who differed in it, and your Lord will certainly judge between them on the Day of Resurrection in those matters in which they used to differ
t.khattab: ˹Honouring˺ the Sabbath was ordained only for those who disputed about Abraham. And surely your Lord will judge between them on the Day of Judgment regarding their disputes
t.ghali: Surely the Sabbath was made only for the ones who differed about it; and surely your Lord will indeed judge between them on the Day of the Resurrection concerning (the things) about which they used to differ
t.irving: The Sabbath was set up only for those who disagreed about it. Your Lord will pass judgment on them on Resurrection Day concerning what- ever they have been disagreeing about
t.iwq: The sabbath was only appointed for those who differed over it. And verily, your Lord will judge between them on the Day of Resurrection concerning that over which they used to differ

t_id.kemenag: Sesungguhnya diwajibkan (menghormati) hari Sabtu atas orang-orang (Yahudi) yang berselisih padanya. Dan sesungguhnya Tuhanmu benar-benar akan memberi putusan di antara mereka di hari kiamat terhadap apa yang telah mereka perselisihkan itu.
t_id.sabiq: Pengagungan hari Jumat--dan bukan hari Sabtu--dalam ajaran Islam sama sekali tidak bertentangan dengan ajaran Ibrâhîm sebagaimana dikatakan oleh orang-orang Yahudi. Larangan berburu pada hari Sabtu, sebagai penghormatan, bukan termasuk syariat Ibrâhîm. Itu hanya berlaku bagi penganut Yahudi saja. Kendati demikian, mereka tetap enggan memberikan penghormatan atas hari itu dan bahkan mereka menyalahi perintah Tuhan. Jika demikian halnya, apa alasan mereka mencaci penganut agama lain yang tidak mau mengagungkan hari Sabtu yang jelas-jelas bukan ajaran agama mereka, sementara orang Yahudi sendiri yang diperintahkan untuk melaksanakan perintah untuk mengagungkan hari Sabtu itu justru mengabaikannya. Yakinlah, wahai Nabi, kelak Allah akan memberikan keputusan pada mereka di hari kiamat berkenaaan dengan persoalan-persoalan yang mereka perselisihkan. Allah akan memberi balasan setiap orang sesuai perbuatan masing-masing.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Sesungguhnya telah dijadikan hari Sabtu) diwajibkan menghormatinya (atas orang-orang Yahudi yang berselisih mengenainya) dengan nabi mereka; mereka adalah orang-orang Yahudi yang diperintahkan oleh Allah supaya mereka menyibukkan dirinya untuk beribadah di hari Jumat, akan tetapi mereka mengatakan, "Kami tidak menghendakinya," lalu mereka memilih hari Sabtu sebagai hari untuk ibadah. Akhirnya Allah memperketat peraturan kepada mereka di hari Sabtu. (Dan sesungguhnya Rabbmu benar-benar akan memberi putusan di antara mereka di hari kiamat terhadap apa yang telah mereka perselisihkan itu) yaitu Dia kelak akan memberi pahala kepada orang yang taat, dan Dia akan mengazab orang-orang yang durhaka melanggar hal-hal yang diharamkan-Nya.
t_id.iwq: Sesungguhnya diwajibkan (menghormati) hari Sabtu atas orang-orang (Yahudi) yang berselisih padanya. Dan sesungguhnya Tuhanmu benar-benar akan memberi putusan di antara mereka di hari kiamat terhadap apa yang telah mereka perselisihkan itu.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «إنما جعل السبت» فرض تعظيمه «على الذين اختلفوا فيه» على نبيهم، وهم اليهود أمروا أن يتفرغوا للعبادة يوم الجمعة فقالوا: لا نريده واختاروا السبت فشدد عليه فيه «وإن ربك ليحكم بينهم يوم القيامة فيما كانوا يختلفون» من أمره بأن يثيب الطائع ويعذب العاصي بانتهاك حرمته
tafsir.siraj-ar: إِنَّمَا جُعِلَ ٱلسَّبۡتُ عَلَى ٱلَّذِينَ ٱخۡتَلَفُواْ فِيهِۚ وَإِنَّ رَبَّكَ لَيَحۡكُمُ بَيۡنَهُمۡ يَوۡمَ ٱلۡقِيَٰمَةِ فِيمَا كَانُواْ فِيهِ يَخۡتَلِفُونَ
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Sesungguhnya telah dijadikan hari Sabtu) diwajibkan menghormatinya (atas orang-orang Yahudi yang berselisih mengenainya) dengan nabi mereka; mereka adalah orang-orang Yahudi yang diperintahkan oleh Allah supaya mereka menyibukkan dirinya untuk beribadah di hari Jumat, akan tetapi mereka mengatakan, "Kami tidak menghendakinya," lalu mereka memilih hari Sabtu sebagai hari untuk ibadah. Akhirnya Allah memperketat peraturan kepada mereka di hari Sabtu. (Dan sesungguhnya Rabbmu benar-benar akan memberi putusan di antara mereka di hari kiamat terhadap apa yang telah mereka perselisihkan itu) yaitu Dia kelak akan memberi pahala kepada orang yang taat, dan Dia akan mengazab orang-orang yang durhaka melanggar hal-hal yang diharamkan-Nya

ar: ٱدۡعُ إِلَىٰ سَبِیلِ رَبِّكَ بِٱلۡحِكۡمَةِ وَٱلۡمَوۡعِظَةِ ٱلۡحَسَنَةِۖ وَجَـٰدِلۡهُم بِٱلَّتِی هِیَ أَحۡسَنُۚ إِنَّ رَبَّكَ هُوَ أَعۡلَمُ بِمَن ضَلَّ عَن سَبِیلِهِۦ وَهُوَ أَعۡلَمُ بِٱلۡمُهۡتَدِینَ
ar_simple: ٱدع إلى سبیل ربك بٱلحكمة وٱلموعظة ٱلحسنة وجدلهم بٱلتی هی أحسن إن ربك هو أعلم بمن ضل عن سبیله وهو أعلم بٱلمهتدین

t.sahih-int: Invite to the way of your Lord with wisdom and good instruction, and argue with them in a way that is best. Indeed, your Lord is most knowing of who has strayed from His way, and He is most knowing of who is [rightly] guided
t.asad: CALL THOU (all mankind] unto thy Sustainer's path with wisdom and goodly exhortation, and argue with them in the most kindly manner- for, behold, thy Sustainer knows best as to who strays from His path, and best knows He as to who are the right-guided
t.yusuf-ali: Invite (all) to the Way of thy Lord with wisdom and beautiful preaching; and argue with them in ways that are best and most gracious: for thy Lord knoweth best, who have strayed from His Path, and who receive guidance
t.pickthall: Call unto the way of thy Lord with wisdom and fair exhortation, and reason with them in the better way. Lo! thy Lord is Best Aware of him who strayeth from His way, and He is Best Aware of those who go aright
t.arberry: Call thou to the way of thy Lord with wisdom and good admonition, and dispute with them in the better way. Surely thy Lord knows very well those who have gone astray from His way, and He knows very well those who are guided
t.hilali-khan: Invite (mankind, O Muhammad SAW) to the Way of your Lord (i.e. Islam) with wisdom (i.e. with the Divine Inspiration and the Quran) and fair preaching, and argue with them in a way that is better. Truly, your Lord knows best who has gone astray from His Path, and He is the Best Aware of those who are guided
t.itani: Invite to the way of your Lord with wisdom and good advice, and debate with them in the most dignified manner. Your Lord is aware of those who stray from His path, and He is aware of those who are guided.
t.mubarakpuri: Invite to the way of your Lord with wisdom and fair preaching, and argue with them with that which is best. Truly, your Lord best knows who has strayed from His path, and He best knows those who are guided.
t.qarib: Call people to the path of your Lord with wisdom and good advice and argue with them in the most courteous way, for your Lord knows best who strays from His path, and knows best who is rightly guided
t.wahiduddin: Call to the way of your Lord with wisdom and fair exhortation and reason with them in a way that is best. Your Lord knows best those who have strayed away from His path, and He knows best those who are rightly guided
t.shakir: Call to the way of your Lord with wisdom and goodly exhortation, and have disputations with them in the best manner; surely your Lord best knows those who go astray from His path, and He knows best those who follow the right way
t.haleem: [Prophet], call [people] to the way of your Lord with wisdom and good teaching. Argue with them in the most courteous way, for your Lord knows best who has strayed from His way and who is rightly guided
t.maariful: Invite (people) to the way of your Lord with wisdom and good counsel. And argue with them in the best of manners. Surely, your Lord knows best the one who deviates from His way, and He knows best the ones who are on the right path
t.khattab: Invite ˹all˺ to the Way of your Lord with wisdom and kind advice, and only debate with them in the best manner. Surely your Lord ˹alone˺ knows best who has strayed from His Way and who is ˹rightly˺ guided
t.ghali: Call (This is addressed to the Prophet) to the way of your Lord with wisdom and fair admonition, and dispute with them in the way (which is) fairest. Surely your Lord is The Best Knower of the ones who have erred away from His way, and He is The Best Knower of the right-guided
t.irving: Invite [people] to your Lord´s way with discretion and kindly instruction, and discuss [things] with them in the politest manner. Your Lord is quite Aware as to who has strayed from His path, just as He is quite Aware of those who have consented to be guided
t.iwq: Invite to the way of your Lord with wisdom and good instruction, and argue with them in a way that is best. Indeed, your Lord is most knowing of who has strayed from His way, and He is most knowing of who is rightly guided

t_id.kemenag: Serulah (manusia) kepada jalan Tuhan-mu dengan hikmah dan pelajaran yang baik dan bantahlah mereka dengan cara yang baik. Sesungguhnya Tuhanmu Dialah yang lebih mengetahui tentang siapa yang tersesat dari jalan-Nya dan Dialah yang lebih mengetahui orang-orang yang mendapat petunjuk.
t_id.sabiq: Wahai Nabi, ajaklah manusia meniti jalan kebenaran yang diperintahkan oleh Tuhanmu. Pilihlah jalan dakwah terbaik yang sesuai dengan kondisi manusia. Ajaklah kaum cendekiawan yang memiliki pengetahuan tinggi untuk berdialog dengan kata-kata bijak, sesuai dengan tingkat kepandaian mereka. Terhadap kaum awam, ajaklah mereka dengan memberikan nasihat dan perumpamaan yang sesuai dengan taraf mereka sehingga mereka sampai kepada kebenaran melalui jalan terdekat yang paling cocok untuk mereka. Debatlah Ahl al-Kitâb yang menganut agama-agama terdahulu dengan logika dan retorika yang halus, melalui perdebatan yang baik, lepas dari kekerasan dan umpatan agar mereka puas dan menerima dengan lapang dada. Itulah metode berdakwah yang benar kepada agama Allah sesuai dengan kecenderungan setiap manusia. Tempuhlah cara itu dalam menghadapi mereka. Sesudah itu serahkan urusan mereka pada Allah yang Maha Mengetahui siapa yang larut dalam kesesatan dan menjauhkan diri dari jalan keselamatan, dan siapa yang sehat jiwanya lalu mendapat petunjuk dan beriman dengan apa yang kamu bawa.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Serulah) manusia, hai Muhammad (kepada jalan Rabbmu) yakni agama-Nya (dengan hikmah) dengan Alquran (dan pelajaran yang baik) pelajaran yang baik atau nasihat yang lembut (dan bantahlah mereka dengan cara) bantahan (yang baik) seperti menyeru mereka untuk menyembah Allah dengan menampilkan kepada mereka tanda-tanda kebesaran-Nya atau dengan hujah-hujah yang jelas. (Sesungguhnya Rabbmu Dialah Yang lebih mengetahui) Maha Mengetahui (tentang siapa yang tersesat dari jalan-Nya dan Dialah yang lebih mengetahui orang-orang yang mendapat petunjuk) maka Dia membalas mereka; ayat ini diturunkan sebelum diperintahkan untuk memerangi orang-orang kafir. Dan diturunkan ketika Hamzah gugur dalam keadaan tercincang; ketika Nabi saw. melihat keadaan jenazahnya, lalu beliau saw. bersumpah melalui sabdanya, "Sungguh aku bersumpah akan membalas tujuh puluh orang dari mereka sebagai penggantimu."
t_id.iwq: Serulah (manusia) kepada jalan Tuhan-mu dengan hikmah dan pelajaran yang baik dan bantahlah mereka dengan cara yang baik. Sesungguhnya Tuhanmu Dialah yang lebih mengetahui tentang siapa yang tersesat dari jalan-Nya dan Dialah yang lebih mengetahui orang-orang yang mendapat petunjuk.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «ادع» الناس يا محمد «إلى سبيل ربك» دينه «بالحكمة» بالقرآن «والموعظة الحسنة» مواعظه أو القول الرقيق «وجادلهم بالتي» أي بالمجادلة التي «هي أحسن» كالدعاء إلى الله بآياته والدعاء إلى حججه «إن ربك هو أعلم» أي عالم «بمن ضل عن سبيله وهو أعلم بالمهتدين» فيجازيهم وهذا قبل الأمر بالقتال ونزل لما قتل حمزة ومثِّل به فقال صلى الله عليه وسلم وقد رآه: لأمثلن بسبعين منهم مكانك
tafsir.siraj-ar: ٱدۡعُ إِلَىٰ سَبِيلِ رَبِّكَ بِٱلۡحِكۡمَةِ وَٱلۡمَوۡعِظَةِ ٱلۡحَسَنَةِۖ وَجَٰدِلۡهُم بِٱلَّتِي هِيَ أَحۡسَنُۚ إِنَّ رَبَّكَ هُوَ أَعۡلَمُ بِمَن ضَلَّ عَن سَبِيلِهِۦ وَهُوَ أَعۡلَمُ بِٱلۡمُهۡتَدِينَ (سَبِيلِ رَبِّكَ: دِينِ رَبِّكَ، وَطَرِيقِهِ المُسْتَقِيم)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Serulah) manusia, hai Muhammad (kepada jalan Rabbmu) yakni agama-Nya (dengan hikmah) dengan Alquran (dan pelajaran yang baik) pelajaran yang baik atau nasihat yang lembut (dan bantahlah mereka dengan cara) bantahan (yang baik) seperti menyeru mereka untuk menyembah Allah dengan menampilkan kepada mereka tanda-tanda kebesaran-Nya atau dengan hujah-hujah yang jelas. (Sesungguhnya Rabbmu Dialah Yang lebih mengetahui) Maha Mengetahui (tentang siapa yang tersesat dari jalan-Nya dan Dialah yang lebih mengetahui orang-orang yang mendapat petunjuk) maka Dia membalas mereka; ayat ini diturunkan sebelum diperintahkan untuk memerangi orang-orang kafir. Dan diturunkan ketika Hamzah gugur dalam keadaan tercincang; ketika Nabi saw. melihat keadaan jenazahnya, lalu beliau saw. bersumpah melalui sabdanya, "Sungguh aku bersumpah akan membalas tujuh puluh orang dari mereka sebagai penggantimu

ar: وَإِنۡ عَاقَبۡتُمۡ فَعَاقِبُوا۟ بِمِثۡلِ مَا عُوقِبۡتُم بِهِۦۖ وَلَئِن صَبَرۡتُمۡ لَهُوَ خَیۡرࣱ لِّلصَّـٰبِرِینَ
ar_simple: وإن عاقبتم فعاقبوا بمثل ما عوقبتم به ولئن صبرتم لهو خیر للصبرین

t.sahih-int: And if you punish [an enemy, O believers], punish with an equivalent of that with which you were harmed. But if you are patient - it is better for those who are patient
t.asad: Hence, if you have to respond to an attack (in argument], respond only to the extent of the attack levelled against you; but to bear yourselves with patience is indeed far better for (you, since God is with] those who are patient in adversity
t.yusuf-ali: And if ye do catch them out, catch them out no worse than they catch you out: But if ye show patience, that is indeed the best (course) for those who are patient
t.pickthall: If ye punish, then punish with the like of that wherewith ye were afflicted. But if ye endure patiently, verily it is better for the patient
t.arberry: And if you chastise, chastise even as you have been chastised; and yet assuredly if you are patient, better it is for those patient
t.hilali-khan: And if you punish (your enemy, O you believers in the Oneness of Allah), then punish them with the like of that with which you were afflicted. But if you endure patiently, verily, it is better for As-Sabirin (the patient ones, etc)
t.itani: If you were to retaliate, retaliate to the same degree as the injury done to you. But if you resort to patience—it is better for the patient.
t.mubarakpuri: And if you punish them, then punish them with the like of that with which you were afflicted. But if you have patience with them, then it is better for those who are patient.
t.qarib: If you want to retaliate to an attack make your response proportionate. But if you can be patient, it is always better to be patient
t.wahiduddin: If you want to retaliate, retaliate to the same degree as the injury done to you. But if you are patient, it is better to be so
t.shakir: And if you take your turn, then retaliate with the like of that with which you were afflicted; but if you are patient, it will certainly be best for those who are patient
t.haleem: If you [believers] have to respond to an attack, make your response proportionate, but it is best to stand fast
t.maariful: And if you were to harm (them) in retaliation, harm them to the measure you were harmed. And if you opt for patience, it is definitely much better for those who are patient
t.khattab: If you retaliate, then let it be equivalent to what you have suffered. But if you patiently endure, it is certainly best for those who are patient
t.ghali: And in case you (This is addressed to all believers) punish, then punish with the like of that wherewith you were punished; and indeed in case you endure patiently, indeed it is more charitable of the patient
t.irving: If you (all) should punish them, then do your punishing to the same extent as you have been punished. Yet if you are patient, well [things go] better for the patient
t.iwq: And if you punish an enemy, O believers, punish with an equivalent of that with which you were harmed. But if you are patient - it is better for those who are patient

t_id.kemenag: Dan jika kamu memberikan balasan, maka balaslah dengan balasan yang sama dengan siksaan yang ditimpakan kepadamu. Akan tetapi jika kamu bersabar, sesungguhnya itulah yang lebih baik bagi orang-orang yang sabar.
t_id.sabiq: Wahai orang-orang Muslim, apabila kalian bermaksud memberi hukuman pada orang yang telah menyakiti kalian, maka berikanlah mereka hukuman yang setimpal dengan kesalahan yang mereka lakukan. Jangan melampaui batas dengan melebihi dari balasan yang sepadan. Kendatipun demikian, yakinlah, jika kalian memilih bersabar dan tidak menuntut balas, maka hal itu akan lebih baik bagi kalian di dunia dan akhirat. Hukumlah mereka dengan tujuan menegakkan kebenaran bukan untuk kepentingan pribadi.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Dan jika kalian memberikan balasan, maka balaslah dengan balasan yang sama dengan siksaan yang ditimpakan kepada kalian. Akan tetapi jika kalian bersabar) tidak mau membalas (sesungguhnya itulah) bersikap sabar itulah (yang lebih baik bagi orang-orang yang sabar) kemudian Nabi saw. membatalkan sumpahnya itu, dan membayar kafaratnya. Demikianlah menurut hadis yang telah diriwayatkan oleh Imam Bazzar.
t_id.iwq: Dan jika kamu memberikan balasan, maka balaslah dengan balasan yang sama dengan siksaan yang ditimpakan kepadamu. Akan tetapi jika kamu bersabar, sesungguhnya itulah yang lebih baik bagi orang-orang yang sabar.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «وإن عاقبتم فعاقبوا بمثل ما عوقبتم به ولئن صبرتم» عن الانتقام «لهو» أي الصبر «خير للصابرين» فكف صلى الله عليه وسلم وكفَّر عن يمينه رواه البزار
tafsir.siraj-ar: وَإِنۡ عَاقَبۡتُمۡ فَعَاقِبُواْ بِمِثۡلِ مَا عُوقِبۡتُم بِهِۦۖ وَلَئِن صَبَرۡتُمۡ لَهُوَ خَيۡرٞ لِّلصَّـٰبِرِينَ
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Dan jika kalian memberikan balasan, maka balaslah dengan balasan yang sama dengan siksaan yang ditimpakan kepada kalian. Akan tetapi jika kalian bersabar) tidak mau membalas (sesungguhnya itulah) bersikap sabar itulah (yang lebih baik bagi orang-orang yang sabar) kemudian Nabi saw. membatalkan sumpahnya itu, dan membayar kafaratnya. Demikianlah menurut hadis yang telah diriwayatkan oleh Imam Bazzar

ar: وَٱصۡبِرۡ وَمَا صَبۡرُكَ إِلَّا بِٱللَّهِۚ وَلَا تَحۡزَنۡ عَلَیۡهِمۡ وَلَا تَكُ فِی ضَیۡقࣲ مِّمَّا یَمۡكُرُونَ
ar_simple: وٱصبر وما صبرك إلا بٱلله ولا تحزن علیهم ولا تك فی ضیق مما یمكرون

t.sahih-int: And be patient, [O Muhammad], and your patience is not but through Allah. And do not grieve over them and do not be in distress over what they conspire
t.asad: Endure, then, with patience (all that they who deny the truth may say] -always remembering that it is none but God who gives thee the strength to endure adversity -and do not grieve over them, and neither be distressed by the false arguments which they devise
t.yusuf-ali: And do thou be patient, for thy patience is but from Allah; nor grieve over them: and distress not thyself because of their plots
t.pickthall: Endure thou patiently (O Muhammad). Thine endurance is only by (the help of) Allah. Grieve not for them, and be not in distress because of that which they devise
t.arberry: And be patient; yet is thy patience only with the help of God. And do not sorrow for them, nor be thou straitened for what they devise
t.hilali-khan: And endure you patiently (O Muhammad SAW), your patience is not but from Allah. And grieve not over them (polytheists and pagans, etc.), and be not distressed because of what they plot
t.itani: So be patient. Your patience is solely from God. And do not grieve over them, and do not be stressed by their schemes.
t.mubarakpuri: And be patient, and your patience will not be, but by the help of Allah. And do not grieve over them, and do not be distressed by their plots.
t.qarib: [Prophet], be patient: your patience comes only from God. Do not grieve over the unbelievers, nor be distressed by their scheming
t.wahiduddin: Endure with patience; truly, your patience is possible only with the help of God. Do not grieve for them, or feel distressed because of their plottings
t.shakir: And be patient and your patience is not but by (the assistance of) Allah, and grieve not for them, and do not distress yourself at what they plan
t.haleem: So [Prophet] be steadfast: your steadfastness comes only from God. Do not grieve over them; do not be distressed by their scheming
t.maariful: And be patient. Your patience is bestowed by none but Allah, and do not grieve over them, and do not be in distress for what they devise
t.khattab: Be patient ˹O Prophet˺, for your patience is only with Allah’s help. Do not grieve over those ˹who disbelieve˺, nor be distressed by their schemes
t.ghali: And (endure) patiently, and in no way is your patience with the help of anyone except with Allah. And do not grieve for them, nor be straitened for whatever they scheme
t.irving: Act patient; your patience comes only from God. Do not feel saddened because of them nor be in any anguish over how they plot
t.iwq: And be patient, O Muhammad, and your patience is not but through Allah. And do not grieve over them and do not be in distress over what they conspire

t_id.kemenag: Bersabarlah (hai Muhammad) dan tiadalah kesabaranmu itu melainkan dengan pertolongan Allah dan janganlah kamu bersedih hati terhadap (kekafiran) mereka dan janganlah kamu bersempit dada terhadap apa yang mereka tipu dayakan.
t_id.sabiq: Demikian pula kamu, wahai Nabi, bersabarlah. Kesabaran itu akan dapat membantu meringankan beban penderitaan dan memecahkan persoalan-persoalan hidup. Janganlah dirimu berduka cita karena keengganan kaummu untuk menerima seruanmu dan beriman kepadamu. Janganlah dadamu merasa sesak oleh makar dan rencana jahat mereka untuk merintangi dakwahmu, karena sesungguhnya perbuatan mereka itu tidak akan pernah mencelakakan dirimu. Sesungguhnya kamu telah mengemban tugas dan bertakwa kepada Tuhanmu.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Bersabarlah dan tiadalah kesabaranmu itu melainkan dengan pertolongan Allah) berkat taufiq-Nya (dan janganlah kamu bersedih hati terhadap kekafiran mereka) terhadap kekafiran orang-orang kafir, jika mereka tidak juga mau beriman, karena kamu menginginkan dengan sangat akan keimanan mereka (dan janganlah kamu bersempit dada terhadap apa yang mereka tipu-dayakan) artinya janganlah engkau hiraukan tipu muslihat mereka, karena sesungguhnya Akulah yang akan menolongmu dalam menghadapi mereka.
t_id.iwq: Bersabarlah (hai Muhammad) dan tiadalah kesabaranmu itu melainkan dengan pertolongan Allah dan janganlah kamu bersedih hati terhadap (kekafiran) mereka dan janganlah kamu bersempit dada terhadap apa yang mereka tipu dayakan.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «واصبر وما صبرك إلا بالله» بتوفيقه «ولا تحزن عليهم» أي الكفار. إن لم يؤمنوا لحرصك على إيمانهم «ولا تك في ضيق مما يمكرون» أي لا تهتم بمكرهم فأنا ناصرك عليهم
tafsir.siraj-ar: وَٱصۡبِرۡ وَمَا صَبۡرُكَ إِلَّا بِٱللَّهِۚ وَلَا تَحۡزَنۡ عَلَيۡهِمۡ وَلَا تَكُ فِي ضَيۡقٖ مِّمَّا يَمۡكُرُونَ
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Bersabarlah dan tiadalah kesabaranmu itu melainkan dengan pertolongan Allah) berkat taufiq-Nya (dan janganlah kamu bersedih hati terhadap kekafiran mereka) terhadap kekafiran orang-orang kafir, jika mereka tidak juga mau beriman, karena kamu menginginkan dengan sangat akan keimanan mereka (dan janganlah kamu bersempit dada terhadap apa yang mereka tipu-dayakan) artinya janganlah engkau hiraukan tipu muslihat mereka, karena sesungguhnya Akulah yang akan menolongmu dalam menghadapi mereka

ar: إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ مَعَ ٱلَّذِینَ ٱتَّقَوا۟ وَّٱلَّذِینَ هُم مُّحۡسِنُونَ
ar_simple: إن ٱلله مع ٱلذین ٱتقوا وٱلذین هم محسنون

t.sahih-int: Indeed, Allah is with those who fear Him and those who are doers of good
t.asad: for, verily, God is with those who are conscious of Him and are doers of good withal
t.yusuf-ali: For Allah is with those who restrain themselves, and those who do good
t.pickthall: Lo! Allah is with those who keep their duty unto Him and those who are doers of good
t.arberry: Surely God is with those who are godfearing, and those who are good-doers
t.hilali-khan: Truly, Allah is with those who fear Him (keep their duty unto Him), and those who are Muhsinun (good-doers, - see the footnote of V)
t.itani: God is with those who are righteous and those who are virtuous.
t.mubarakpuri: Truly, Allah is with those who have Taqwa, and the doers of good.
t.qarib: for, truly, God stands with those who are mindful of Him and who do good
t.wahiduddin: for God is with those who are righteous and those who do good
t.shakir: Surely Allah is with those who guard (against evil) and those who do good (to others)
t.haleem: for God is with those who are mindful of Him and who do good
t.maariful: Surely, Allah is with those who fear Him and those who are good in their deeds
t.khattab: Surely Allah is with those who shun evil and who do good ˹deeds˺
t.ghali: Surely Allah is with the ones who have been pious and the ones who are fair-doers
t.irving: God stands alongside those who fulfill their duty and who act kindly
t.iwq: Indeed, Allah is with those who fear Him and those who are doers of good

t_id.kemenag: Sesungguhnya Allah beserta orang-orang yang bertakwa dan orang-orang yang berbuat kebaikan.
t_id.sabiq: Sesungguhnya Tuhanmu selalu menyertai orang-orang yang menjaga diri dari murka-Nya, dengan cara menjauhkan diri dari larangan-Nya dan berbuat baik dengan menaati semua perintah-Nya. Allah akan selalu memberikan pertolongan pada mereka di dunia dan akan memberikan balasan yang baik di akhirat.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Sesungguhnya Allah beserta orang-orang yang bertakwa) orang-orang yang takut terhadap kekafiran dan kemaksiatan (dan orang-orang yang berbuat kebaikan) dengan menjalankan ketaatan, kesabaran; Allah akan menolong mereka dengan bantuan dan pertolongan-Nya.
t_id.iwq: Sesungguhnya Allah beserta orang-orang yang bertakwa dan orang-orang yang berbuat kebaikan.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «إن الله مع الذين اتقوا» الكفر والمعاصي «والذين هم محسنون» بالطاعة والصبر، بالعون والنصر
tafsir.siraj-ar: إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ مَعَ ٱلَّذِينَ ٱتَّقَواْ وَّٱلَّذِينَ هُم مُّحۡسِنُونَ
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Sesungguhnya Allah beserta orang-orang yang bertakwa) orang-orang yang takut terhadap kekafiran dan kemaksiatan (dan orang-orang yang berbuat kebaikan) dengan menjalankan ketaatan, kesabaran; Allah akan menolong mereka dengan bantuan dan pertolongan-Nya
